entries.definition:
كتاب الظاء

ظهر

(باب الظاء والهاء وما يثلثهما)

(ظهر) الظاء والهاء والراء أصلٌ صحيحٌ واحدٌ يدلُّ على قوّةٍ وبروز. من ذلك ظَهَرَ الشيءُ يظهرُ ظهوراً فهو ظاهر، إذا انكشفَ وبرزَ. ولذلك سمِّيَ وقت الظُّهرِ والظَّهيرة، وهو أظهر أوقات النّهار وأَضْوَؤُها. والأصل فيه كلّه ظهر الإنسان، وهو خلافُ بطنه، وهو يجمع البُروزَ والقوّة. ويقال للرِّكاب الظَّهر، لأنَّ الذي يَحمِل منها الشيءَ ظهورُها. ويقال رجلٌ مظهَّر، أي شديد الظَّهْر. ورجلٌ ظَهِر : يشتكي ظهره.

ومن الباب: أظهرْنا، إذا سرنا في وقت الظُّهْرِ. ومنه: ظهرتُ على كذا، إذا اطلَّعتَ عليه. والظَّهِير: البعير القويّ. والظَّهيرِ: المُعين، كأنَّه أسندَ ظَهْرَه إلى ظهرك. والظُّهور: الغَلبة. قال الله تعالى: ] فَأَصْبَحُوا ظَاهِرِين [ [الصف 14]. والظَّاهرة: العين الجاحظة. والظِّهار: قولُ الرَّجل لامرأته: أنتِ عَلَيَّ كظهرِ أُمِّي. وهي كلمةٌ كانوا يقولونها، يريدونَ بها الفراق. وإِنَّما اختصُّوا الظَّهْر لمكان الرُّكوب، وإلاَّ فسائر أعضائِها في التَّحريمِ كالظَّهْرِ. والظُّهار من الرِّيش: ما يظهر منه في الجَناح. والظِّهريُّ: كلُّ شيءٍ تجعله بظَهْرٍ ، أي تنساه، كأَنَّكَ قد جعلتَه خلف ظهرِكَ ، إِعراضاً عنه وتركاً لـه. قال الله سبحانه: ] وَاتّخَذْتُمُوهُ وَرَاءَكُمْ ظِهْرِيّاً [ [هود 92]. وقد جعل فلانٌ حاجتي بظهرٍ، إذا لم يُقْبِل عليها، بل جعلها وراءه. وقال الفرزدق:

[remember] Dhūʾl-Nūn [Jonah], when he went off angrily, and assumed We would not have power over him 4 to conjecture, to guess (45:32) مَا نَدْرِي مَا السَّاعَةُ إِن نَّظُنُّ إِلَّا ظَنًّا we do not know what the Hour is; we only hazard a guess 5 to fear (7:171) وَإِذْ نَتَقْنَا الْجَبَلَ فَوْقَهُمْ كَأَنَّهُ ظُلَّةٌ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُ وَاقِعٌ بِهِمْ and [remember] when We hoisted the mountain high above them as if it were a canopy, and they feared that it would fall on them 6 to expect (18:35) مَا أَظُنُّ أَن تَبِيدَ هَذِهِ أَبَدًا do not think this will ever perish 7 [with prep. بِ] to think ill of, to impute to (3:154) يَظُنُّونَ بِاللَّهِ غَيْرَ الْحَقِّ ظَنَّ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ imputing [ill] thoughts about God that are contrary to the truth, the thoughts of pagan ignorance.
ظَنّ ẓann [v. n./n.; pl. ظُنُون ẓunūn] 1 conjecture, speculation (4:157) مَا لَهُم بِهِ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِلَّا اتِّبَاعَ الظَّنِّ they have no real knowledge of it, only following conjecture 2 assumption (38:27) ذَٰلِكَ ظَنُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا that is the disbelievers’ assumption 3 thought (48:12) وَظَنَنتُمْ ظَنَّ السَّوْءِ and you thought evil thoughts; *(33:10) وَتَظُنُّونَ بِاللَّهِ الظُّنُونَا and you imputed [ill] thoughts [lit. various thoughts] to God 4 expectation, opinion (34:20) وَلَقَدْ صَدَّقَ عَلَيْهِمْ إِبْلِيسُ ظَنَّهُ فَاتَّبَعُوهُ Satan proved right his opinion of them for they followed him.

ظَانِّين ẓānnīn [pl. of act. part. ظَانّ ẓānn] those who think, doubt, conjecture, assume, presume, expect, hold an opinion (48:6) الظَّانِّينَ بِاللَّهِ ظَنَّ السَّوْءِ those who harbour evil thoughts about God.
entries.definition_english:
[remember] Dhūʾl-Nūn [Jonah], when he went off angrily, and assumed We would not have power over him 4 to conjecture, to guess (45:32) مَا نَدْرِي مَا السَّاعَةُ إِن نَّظُنُّ إِلَّا ظَنًّا we do not know what the Hour is; we only hazard a guess 5 to fear (7:171) وَإِذْ نَتَقْنَا الْجَبَلَ فَوْقَهُمْ كَأَنَّهُ ظُلَّةٌ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُ وَاقِعٌ بِهِمْ and [remember] when We hoisted the mountain high above them as if it were a canopy, and they feared that it would fall on them 6 to expect (18:35) مَا أَظُنُّ أَن تَبِيدَ هَذِهِ أَبَدًا do not think this will ever perish 7 [with prep. بِ] to think ill of, to impute to (3:154) يَظُنُّونَ بِاللَّهِ غَيْرَ الْحَقِّ ظَنَّ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ imputing [ill] thoughts about God that are contrary to the truth, the thoughts of pagan ignorance.

entries.definition:
ẓ-h-r back, rear; backer, to back up; to neglect, to turn one’s back on; to carry on one’s back; outside, exterior, external, outward; to be apparent, perceptible, manifest, plain, evident; to assist, to gang up on; appearances; to travel from one place to another; to overpower, to conquer; noon, midday. Of this root, 12 forms occur 59 times in the Qurʾan: ظَهَرَ ẓahara 10 times; ظَاهَرَ ẓāhara six times; أَظْهَرَ ʾaẓhara seven times; تَظَاهَرَ taẓāhara three times; ظُهُور zuhūr 11 times; ظَهْر ẓahr four times; ظَاهِر ẓāhir six times; ظَاهِرُون ẓāhirūn twice; ظَاهِرَة ẓāhiratun twice; ظَاهِرِين ẓāhirīn twice; ظَهِير ẓahīr six times; ظَهِيرَة ẓahīratun once and ظِهْرِيًّا ẓihriyyan once.
ظَهَرَ ẓahara a I [v. intrans.] 1 to appear, to become evident, to become manifest (30:41) ظَهَرَ الْفَسَادُ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ corruption has appeared on land and sea 2 to be open, to be manifest (6:151) وَلَا

تقربوا الفواحش ما ظهر منها وما بطن and do not approach indecent deeds, be they open or secret 3 to triumph, to spread out (9:48) حتى جاء الحق وظهر أمر الله until the truth came and God’s command triumphed 4 to show (24:31) ولا يبدين زينتهن إلا ما ظهر منها and should not flaunt their charms beyond those of them that may [unavoidably/decently] show 5 [with prep. على] a) to ascend (43:33) ومعارج عليها يظهرون and staircases upon which they ascend b) to prevail over, to get the upper hand over (9:8) إن يظهروا عليكم لا يرقبوا فيكم إلا ولا ذمة if they get the upper hand over you, they would not respect any tie with you, of kinship or treaty c) to come to know, to become cognisant of (24:31) أو التابعين غير أولي الإربة من الرجال أو الطفل الذين لم يظهروا على عورات النساء or such men as attend them who have no sexual desire or children who are not yet aware of women’s nakedness d) to discover, to find out, to come upon (9:8) إن يظهروا عليكم يرجموكم أو يعيدوكم في ملتهم if they discover you they would stone you or force you to return to their religion II [v. trans.] to climb on the back of, to scale (18:97) فما استطاعوا أن يظهروه so they could not scale it.

ظاهر ẓāhara [v. III] I [intrans.] [jur.] to perform ظهار ẓihār, i.e., to say to one’s wife (according to a pre-Islamic practice) that her back is as forbidden to one as the back of one’s mother (33:4) وما جعل أزواجكم اللائي تظاهرون منهن أمهاتكم and He has not made your wives, whom you declare to be ‘unlawful to you as your mothers’ backs’, your [real] mothers II [trans.] to back up, to aid, to support (9:4) ولم يظاهروا عليكم أحدا and who have not supported anyone against you.

أظهر ʾaẓhara [v. IV] I [intrans.] to enter into the noontime, midday (30:18) وله الحمد في السموات والأرض وعشيا وحين تظهرون praise is due to Him everywhere [lit. in the heavens and on earth] all the time [lit. in the evening and at midday] II [trans.] 1 to cause to spread (40:26) أو أن يظهر في الأرض الفساد or that he will cause disorder to spread in the land 2 to reveal to, to make known to, to divulge (72:26) عالم الغيب فلا يظهر على غيبه أحدا the One who knows what is hidden—He reveals to none His secrets 3 to cause to prevail, to cause to overcome, to exalt (48:28) هو الذي أرسل رسوله بالهدى ودين الحق ليظهره على الدين كله it is He who sent His Messenger

591
